content:
# Genesis Chapter 5 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 3v-4r | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/GEN_005_page_001.jpg, GEN_005_page_002.jpg
**Additional Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209) — LXX | **View Vaticanus online:** https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209

---

**5:1** This is the scroll of the generations of Adam [סֵפֶר תּוֹלְדֹת אָדָם]. In the day God created Adam, in the likeness [בִּדְמוּת] of God he made him.

**5:2** Male and female he created them, and he blessed them, and he called their name Adam [אָדָם] in the day they were created.

**5:3** And Adam lived one hundred and thirty years, and he begot in his likeness [בִּדְמוּתוֹ], according to his image [כְּצַלְמוֹ], and he called his name Shet [שֵׁת].

**5:4** And the days of Adam after he begot Shet were eight hundred years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:5** And all the days of Adam that he lived were nine hundred and thirty years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:6** And Shet lived one hundred and five years, and he begot Enosh [אֱנוֹשׁ].

**5:7** And Shet lived after he begot Enosh eight hundred and seven years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:8** And all the days of Shet were nine hundred and twelve years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:9** And Enosh lived ninety years, and he begot Qenan [קֵינָן].

**5:10** And Enosh lived after he begot Qenan eight hundred and fifteen years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:11** And all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:12** And Qenan lived seventy years, and he begot Mahalalel [מַהֲלַלְאֵל].

**5:13** And Qenan lived after he begot Mahalalel eight hundred and forty years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:14** And all the days of Qenan were nine hundred and ten years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:15** And Mahalalel lived sixty and five years, and he begot Yered [יָרֶד].

**5:16** And Mahalalel lived after he begot Yered eight hundred and thirty years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:17** And all the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred and ninety and five years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:18** And Yered lived one hundred and sixty and two years, and he begot Hanokh [חֲנוֹךְ].

**5:19** And Yered lived after he begot Hanokh eight hundred years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:20** And all the days of Yered were nine hundred and sixty and two years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:21** And Hanokh lived sixty and five years, and he begot Metushelah [מְתוּשֶׁלַח].

**5:22** And Hanokh walked [וַיִּתְהַלֵּךְ] with the-God [אֶת-הָאֱלֹהִים] after he begot Metushelah three hundred years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:23** And all the days of Hanokh were three hundred and sixty and five years.

**5:24** And-walked [וַיִּתְהַלֵּךְ] Hanokh with-the-God [אֶת-הָאֱלֹהִים], and-he-was-not [וְאֵינֶנּוּ], for took [כִּי-לָקַח] him God [אֹתוֹ אֱלֹהִים].

**5:25** And Metushelah lived one hundred and eighty and seven years, and he begot Lemekh [לֶמֶךְ].

**5:26** And Metushelah lived after he begot Lemekh seven hundred and eighty and two years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:27** And all the days of Metushelah were nine hundred and sixty and nine years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:28** And Lemekh lived one hundred and eighty and two years, and he begot a son.

**5:29** And he called his name Noach [נֹחַ], saying: This one will comfort [יְנַחֲמֵנוּ] us from our work and from the toil of our hands, from the ground [הָאֲדָמָה] that YHWH cursed.

**5:30** And Lemekh lived after he begot Noach five hundred and ninety and five years, and he begot sons and daughters.

**5:31** And all the days of Lemekh were seven hundred and seventy and seven years, and he died [וַיָּמֹת].

**5:32** And Noach was a son of five hundred years, and Noach begot Shem [שֵׁם], Ham [חָם], and Yaphet [יָפֶת].

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### On the Toledot Formula (v. 1)

Verse 1 opens the second toledot formula of Genesis: סֵפֶר תּוֹלְדֹת אָדָם, "the scroll of the generations of Adam." Unlike the first toledot in 2:4 (תּוֹלְדוֹת הַשָּׁמַיִם וְהָאָרֶץ), this one is explicitly called a סֵפֶר ("scroll/book"), suggesting a document-like formality. The echo of 1:26-27 is deliberate: בִּדְמוּת אֱלֹהִים עָשָׂה אֹתוֹ ("in the likeness of God he made him") recalls the creation mandate.

### On "Adam" as Name and Category (v. 2)

In v. 2 the text reads וַיִּקְרָא אֶת-שְׁמָם אָדָם, "and he called their name Adam." The word אָדָם functions simultaneously as proper name and generic category ("humankind"). The LXX renders this as Αδαμ, treating it as a proper name. No article appears in either verse.

### On the Reversed Image-Likeness Order (v. 3)

In 1:26 God says נַעֲשֶׂה אָדָם בְּצַלְמֵנוּ כִּדְמוּתֵנוּ — tselem first, then demut. In 5:3, Adam begets Seth בִּדְמוּתוֹ כְּצַלְמוֹ — demut first, then tselem. The reversal is not an error; it is a literary signal. The divine creative order (image then likeness) is mirrored and inverted in human procreation (likeness then image), marking the transmission of both glory and mortality down the line.

### On Enoch (vv. 22-24)

The construction in v. 22 and v. 24 uses the Hitpael of הָלַךְ (הִתְהַלֵּךְ), translated "walked" but carrying the reflexive-iterative sense: "kept walking," "walked about." The phrase אֶת-הָאֱלֹהִים ("with the-God") uses the definite article on אֱלֹהִים, rare and emphatic — not "with God" in the generic sense but "with the [true] God." The fourfold structure of v. 24 is preserved exactly in the translation:

> And-walked / Hanokh / with-the-God / and-he-was-not / for-took / him / God.

וְאֵינֶנּוּ is a compound negative (אֵין + pronoun suffix 3ms), meaning "and he was not [there]" — not simply "he was no more." The verb לָקַח ("took") requires no object other than אֹתוֹ, making God the unambiguous subject of a divine act of taking.

### On the "And He Died" Refrain

The phrase וַיָּמֹת appears at the end of the lifespan summary for Adam, Seth, Enosh, Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared, Methuselah, Lamech — eight times in all. Enoch alone is exempt. The monotonous repetition is not stylistic weakness but theological argument: death is the structural constant of post-fall human existence. The eighth patriarch's absence from the refrain is, by contrast, thunderous.

### On Lamech's Word Play (v. 29)

The name נֹחַ (Noach) is connected etymologically to the root נחם (nacham, "to comfort/relieve") via the verb יְנַחֲמֵנוּ ("he will comfort us"). The phonological connection is approximate — Noach derives more directly from נוּחַ ("to rest") — but the narrator deploys the wordplay intentionally. The reference to the cursed ground (הָאֲדָמָה) ties back to 3:17, anchoring this genealogy in the consequences of the fall.

---

### Chronology Comparison Table: Masoretic Text (Leningrad Codex) vs. LXX (Codex Vaticanus)

The most significant textual divergence between the Hebrew (MT) and Greek (LXX) traditions in Genesis 5 is a systematic addition of approximately 100 years to the pre-firstborn ages of the patriarchs in the LXX, with corresponding reductions in the post-firstborn ages so that total lifespans remain broadly similar. The Samaritan Pentateuch (SP) represents a third tradition with its own variants, noted where relevant.

| Patriarch | MT: Age at firstborn | LXX: Age at firstborn | Difference | MT: Post-firstborn | LXX: Post-firstborn | MT: Total | LXX: Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Adam | 130 | 230 | +100 LXX | 800 | 700 | 930 | 930 |
| Seth | 105 | 205 | +100 LXX | 807 | 707 | 912 | 912 |
| Enosh | 90 | 190 | +100 LXX | 815 | 715 | 905 | 905 |
| Kenan | 70 | 170 | +100 LXX | 840 | 740 | 910 | 910 |
| Mahalalel | 65 | 165 | +100 LXX | 830 | 730 | 895 | 895 |
| Jared | 162 | 162 | 0 | 800 | 800 | 962 | 962 |
| Enoch | 65 | 165 | +100 LXX | 300 | 200 | 365 | 365 |
| Methuselah | 187 | 187 | 0 | 782 | 782 | 969 | 969 |
| Lamech | 182 | 188 | +6 LXX | 595 | 565 | 777 | 753 |
| Noah at flood | — | — | — | — | — | 500 (at sons) | 500 (at sons) |

**Notes on the table:**

1. **The +100 pattern** applies consistently to Adam through Mahalalel and to Enoch, but not to Jared or Methuselah. This is a recognized feature of the LXX tradition, likely reflecting either a different Vorlage (Hebrew base text) or deliberate scribal adjustment. The most plausible scholarly explanation is that the LXX (or its Hebrew source) added 100 years pre-firstborn to prevent a younger patriarch from appearing to have been born after an older patriarch's death — a calendrical-genealogical harmonization.

2. **Total lifespans preserved:** The LXX compensates by reducing post-firstborn years by ~100 in most cases, so the grand totals are identical for Adam through Jared and Enoch. This internal consistency suggests intentionality rather than scribal error.

3. **Methuselah's anomaly:** In the LXX chronology, Methuselah survives the flood by approximately 14 years (he would have died in 2256 AM vs. the flood in 2242 AM). This is a well-known problem in LXX scholarship. The MT chronology avoids this: Methuselah dies in the year of the flood (1656 AM). Some LXX manuscripts attempted corrections.

4. **Lamech's variant (+6):** The LXX gives Lamech 188 years before fathering Noah vs. MT's 182 — a smaller discrepancy that does not fit the clean +100 pattern and may reflect independent textual tradition.

5. **Source for LXX figures:** Verified against the Vaticanus image (Vat.gr.1209, folio 4, Genesis 5) and standard critical apparatus (Wevers, *Genesis*, Göttingen Septuaginta, 1974).

---

*Signed: EMET (אֱמֶת — Truth)*
*Translation from Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 3v-4r, photographed by the West Semitic Research Project (© Bruce E. Zuckerman).*
*LXX consultation: Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana.*
